Rupee falls 30 paise to 94.63 against Dollar amid Middle East uncertainty
description: The Indian rupee weakened by 30 paise to 94.63 against the US dollar on Monday, pressured by a strong dollar and Middle East tensions. Despite supportive inflows and falling crude oil prices, importer demand and foreign institutional investors turning net sellers weighed on the currency. While equity markets saw gains, core sector growth slowed, and the RBI actively intervened in the forex market.

title: ‘Mujhe mere bete ke paas jaane do’: Grief and horror outside Lucknow fire site
description: Tragedy struck Lucknow's Aliganj as a devastating fire ripped through a three-storey commercial complex, claiming 15 young lives, mostly students at an animation centre. Families received frantic calls as thick smoke and flames engulfed the building. Brave locals and first responders risked their lives in desperate rescue attempts, while firefighters battled the blaze. The incident has left many grieving and seeking answers.

title: India vs England T20Is: Coles gets maiden call-up in 17-member squad
description: England have named a 17-man squad for the five-match T20I series against India, handing all-rounder James Coles his maiden call-up. Harry Brook will captain the side, with Jordan Cox, Sonny Baker and Saqib Mahmood returning. Injuries rule out Brydon Carse and Jamie Overton. The series begins on July 1, with Jos Buttler and Jofra Archer among the key names.

15 dead in Lucknow fire: Building was residential on paper; 16 officials under scanner
description: A deadly Aliganj fire investigation reveals the building was approved for residential use but later converted to a commercial complex. Officials are probing lapses by 16 individuals, including former employees, for alleged negligence in monitoring and approvals. The Lucknow Municipal Corporation had been collecting commercial property tax since 2022, indicating awareness of its use. The probe aims to uncover administrative negligence and regulatory violations contributing to the tragedy.

title: In Pune Municipal Corporation, opposition wears helmets citing 'threat' from BJP
description: Pune Municipal Corporation proceedings were disrupted by opposition corporators protesting alleged BJP aggression with construction helmets and black armbands. This follows a chaotic previous meeting. Meanwhile, Uddhav Thackeray indicated he would soon address his party's recent setback, with six Lok Sabha MPs defecting to Eknath Shinde's Shiv Sena. Shinde welcomed the MPs, calling it a continuation of his movement to preserve Balasaheb Thackeray's ideology.

description: A devastating fire swept through a coaching-cum-gaming zone in Lucknow's Aliganj area on Monday afternoon, claiming the lives of at least 12 young students. Fourteen fire tenders battled the blaze that erupted around 3 pm. While authorities are investigating the exact origin, the incident has sent shockwaves through the city, with many others reportedly injured in the tragedy.

title: The new missile order: How India is changing the way it builds its precision arsenal
description: Missiles are no longer niche assets reserved for select targets. They are increasingly becoming consumable instruments of warfare, used repeatedly to suppress air defences, destroy logistics hubs, disrupt command networks and shape battlefield conditions. The challenge, therefore, is no longer limited to developing sophisticated missiles. A production system dependent on a single dominant manufacturer can struggle to meet those demands.

Criminals are using forged documents, fake online exams, and impersonations of high-ranking officials like IAS and IPS officers to swindle aspirants. These elaborate frauds, often involving significant financial and emotional investment, highlight a growing trend of sophisticated cybercrime targeting hopeful individuals across India.

Property inherited from parents: Check if you need to pay taxes on it
description: Inheriting property in India comes without inheritance tax, but beware of future taxation responsibilities. Local municipal bodies will impose an annual property tax. If you choose to rent out your inherited estate, remember that the rental income is taxable, albeit with possible deductions available. Should you sell the property, capital gains tax will apply, calculated on the original owner's acquisition cost, with distinct rates for both short-term and long-term profits.

description: India is in advanced discussions with the UAE for a significant defence deal, potentially including the sale of BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles and Akashteer air defence systems. This move signifies deepening strategic ties and India's growing prowess as a defence exporter. The UAE's interest stems from a desire to bolster its military capabilities amidst regional security concerns, marking a potential major win for India's indigenous defence industry.

Panthers, Chargers eye Eagles' $21M star as NFL trade market sees major reset
description: Jalen Carter has emerged as a major name in NFL trade rumors after Jeffery Simmons signed a massive $105 million extension that could reshape the market for elite defensive tackles. With Carter still playing on his four-year, $21.8 million rookie contract, speculation is growing around whether the Philadelphia Eagles will pay up or explore trade options. The Carolina Panthers and Los Angeles Chargers have surfaced as possible landing spots.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
description: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.

title: Two of UK's biggest telcos roll out 'kill switch' to block stolen phones from working
description: UK mobile giants Virgin Media O2 and VodafoneThree have activated a new 'kill switch' for new phones stolen from their stores, aiming to cripple the black market for stolen devices. This move follows resistance from major manufacturers like Apple and Samsung to a universal anti-theft lock. The technology renders stolen, unsold handsets unusable remotely, a measure previously seen in the Netherlands.
